The Ask
Phase 3 of Baton's AV refresh. Conference rooms are done. This is the all-hands space: TGIT, full-company meetings, and hybrid demos. It needs to work reliably for everyone in the room and on the call.
Three things we already agree on
1
Hybrid meetings are a KTR priority. Right now, remote participants in TGIT consistently struggle to hear and be heard. That's the problem this solves.
2
Any AV investment we make should be able to move with us. We don't want to pay twice if Baton relocates in the next few years.
3
We want the space ready before OKR week on July 13. That's the first major use case and it needs to work.
Our upstairs neighbor recently completed a similar setup. This is what a large-format TV looks like in a comparable space.
A sense of how a 98" screen fits within our all-hands space dimensions.
Why CTI
Two vendors were fully evaluated. CTI is the right choice: platform-agnostic, IP-based, built to move with us, and proactive support included from day one.
Recommendation: CTI at $48,269.38 (updated May 12, 2026)
Out of 2 vendors fully evaluated, CTI is our clear recommendation. The system is fully portable (IP-based, nothing stays in the walls), supports any meeting platform, includes a Logitech Tap IP touchpad for one-tap room join, and 1-yr proactive support is included at no extra cost.
Key equipment
Logitech Rally Bar
Front of room — audience view
Logitech Rally Camera
Facing presenter — presenter view
Logitech Tap IP
One-tap room controller — Teams or Google Meet
Newline 98" STV+ TV
98" 4K commercial display — included in base quote
Shure MXW1X Wireless Lavalier
Wireless bodypack + lavalier mic — 2 included for presenters
QSC AC-C6T Ceiling Speaker
4 ceiling speakers for even full-room audio coverage
What CTI delivers — mapped to your original requirements
✓
Platform-agnostic BYOD system
Anyone plugs in via USB-C or HDMI. The room recognizes their laptop as a camera, mic, and speaker — and works with Teams, Google Meet, Zoom, or Webex. No platform lock-in. Once you choose a platform, CTI adds a dedicated room touchpad for one-tap join.
Grant's initial ask: "Standardizing on a single technology solution for hybrid meetings"
✓
Camera coverage: Logitech Rally Camera + Rally Bar
The updated quote includes a Logitech Rally Camera Streamline Kit and a Logitech Rally Bar + Tap IP Bundle. The Rally Bar has a built-in camera; the Rally Camera adds a second view.
Grant's initial ask: "Remote staff should be able to present to the local and remote audience"
✓
Ceiling speakers with full-room coverage
Four QSC ceiling speakers distribute audio evenly across the space. No dead spots. Remote participants and local audio both come through clearly from any seat.
Grant's initial ask: "Speakers should be audible through the TGIT space"
✓
Wireless lavalier mics plus Catchbox audience mic
Two Shure wireless bodypacks for presenters — move freely, no podium required. Catchbox (Baton-owned) for audience participation and Q&A. Acoustic echo cancellation and automatic mic mixing built in. Handheld mic can be added to scope as needed.
Grant's initial ask: "Support for at least 4 microphones. The presenter would ideally be able to walk around."
✓
IP-based architecture: fully portable
Everything runs over the network. Camera, speakers, display, DSP — all connected via standard Cat6 cable. If Baton moves, we unplug the network switch and all devices, load them on a truck, and reconnect at the new space. No cables stay in the walls.
Grant's initial ask: protecting the investment against a future office move
✓
Touch panel control — anyone can run the room
Tabletop QSC touch panel handles volume, mic mute, camera presets, and power. No IT support needed to run a meeting. Pre-configured camera presets (presenter, audience, full room) make it straightforward for any host.
Grant's initial ask: "The TGIT space should be a meeting room that is reservable in our calendar system"
✓
1-yr CTI Complete proactive support — included
Remote monitoring, on-demand service, a dedicated care team, and critical meeting support for the first year. Young Electric includes no ongoing support.
Grant's initial ask: reliability for TGIT and all-hands events without depending on ad hoc IT fixes
Young Electric: hardwired, no support included
Young Electric's system is hardwired — speaker and camera cables stay in the building if Baton moves. No ongoing support is included. Young is ~$4,248 less ($44,021 vs. $48,269.38), has no upfront deposit, and is a local SF contractor. It remains a viable fallback only if the CTI deposit becomes a cash flow blocker.

Some comparison details are based on vendor materials and have not been independently verified.
Alignment Needed
A few areas where we'd like your input before moving forward. CTI is the recommendation — these are the remaining details to align on.
Platform: Teams or Google Meet?
Alignment Needed
Recommended Microsoft Teams — Ryder is already on Teams, which simplifies Ryder-Baton meeting join and matches your original AV requirements. Requires a Teams Room license (~$480/yr) and IT coordination with Ryder to provision the room account. Strongest alignment with the Ryder relationship. Will stay consistent with our current setup in our conference rooms.
Option B Google Meet — Baton's primary internal platform. No room license fee. Simpler to set up independently from Ryder IT. The tradeoff: Ryder participants join through Teams, not Google Meet, so there's no unified room controller experience for Ryder-facing meetings.
Display: 98" TV or projector?
Alignment Needed
Rec. 98" commercial TV — included in base quote. Works in ambient light without dimming. Fully portable if Baton moves. No electrical work, no landlord coordination, no bulb replacements.
Alt. Projector + screen — more of an event feel. Downsides: requires dimming, electrical work, light removal, likely landlord approval, not portable, adds ~$8,500 — which puts us over budget.
Gaming area: where does it go?
No decision needed today
The current all-hands space doubles as the gaming area. For now, the recommendation is to keep everything in place and plan the gaming area move once the AV setup is complete. Options are below for when we're ready to revisit.
Start here Keep in same space — no disruption during install. We'll plan the gaming area move as a separate workstream once the AV system is live and we can properly assess capacity needs.
Future option Near the front door — cleanest long-term separation. All-hands space becomes fully dedicated. Gaming becomes a culture signal at the entrance for visitors and guests.
Future option Dining room — we would not have space for the pool table and would need to figure out gaming with the current table and banquette seating setup.
Timeline & Budget
Working backward from July 6. The timeline is achievable but has no slack. A fallback plan is in place if install timing slips past the deadline.
Project timeline
Complete
Vendor research and comparison
Two full quotes received and evaluated. CTI selected as recommendation.
Now — awaiting your approval
Leadership decisions
Vendor, platform, display, gaming area, and budget. Updated CTI quote to follow once platform is decided.
End of this week or early next week
Sign with CTI and initiate deposit
60% deposit (~$28,073) triggers procurement. Exact timing depends on which products CTI orders and delivery lead times. Earlier signing = more install buffer.
Parallel track
Room prep and furniture removal
Furniture cleared and space prepped ahead of CTI's arrival.
TBD — confirm with CTI after signing
CTI installation and tuning
CTI estimates up to one week for installation, potentially less. Loudest work (drilling, cable runs) can be scheduled on Mondays or Fridays to limit disruption to the team. Target: schedule install during remote week (June 8–12) if the timeline aligns with CTI — minimal team impact. CTI provides configuration, tuning, and end-user training on completion.
Target: July 6
Space ready for all-hands use
Full hybrid-ready system operational. First major use case: TGIT and OKR week starting July 13.
Fallback plan if install timing slips
Once product ETAs are confirmed, Chelsea will check in with CTI to schedule install. If the full install can't be completed by July 6, we default to after OKR week — the current all-hands setup in the family room stays in place with no disruption to lunch or daily use.

All-in estimate~$55,000
CTI quote confirmed at $48,269.38. ~$6,700 remaining covers contingency and pre-signing items. Future add-ons are phased in separately.
60% deposit due on signing (~$28,962)
Due immediately upon signing with CTI to initiate procurement. Remaining 40% (~$19,308) invoiced on completion. Flag with Brett before committing.
Risks & Next Steps
Flagged risks that could affect scope, cost, or timeline. Most are Chelsea's to resolve before signing. Nothing here should block approval.
Action Required
Budget nearly fully committed — add-ons are a separate conversation
CTI quote ($48,269.38) + 10% contingency (~$4,827) + wall backing (~$500–$1,500) accounts for ~$53,600–$54,600 of the $55K all-in budget. Handheld mic, rugs, furniture, and other enhancements are intentionally phased as future add-ons — not part of this approval ask. Any in-scope additions require a budget increase.
Watch
Product delivery timelines affect how quickly we can move
Which products CTI orders affects lead times. Signing ideally happens end of this week or early next week. Every week of delay compresses the install window with no buffer before July 6.
Watch
Change orders could add cost if unexpected issues arise on-site
CTI's contract includes provisions for change orders triggered by overtime, weekend work, or unexpected discoveries during install. A ~10% contingency (~$4,827) is already built into the $55K all-in budget to cover this.
Resolved
Quote is all-inclusive — no hidden costs
CTI confirmed the quote covers shipping, freight, tariffs, equipment, and installation in full. No additional line items expected.
Resolved
July 6 install window is contractually protected
CTI will not begin unless they can guarantee completion before July 6. If they can't commit, installation will not start until after OKR week — no partial installs, no disruption.
Resolved
Network compatibility
CTI's system runs AV-over-IP and requires low-latency network support. Baton recently upgraded its network, so this is expected to be a non-issue. Martin (Jones IT) to confirm before install as a precaution.
Note
Projector option would exceed budget and require landlord approval
If projector is chosen instead of the TV, the ~$8,500 add-on cost would push us over the $55K all-in budget. It would also require electrical work, light removal, and likely landlord coordination. The TV avoids all of this and is the recommendation.
